title_en: Sugar: The Sweet Obsession Fueled by Regulation
title_es: Azúcar: La Obsesión Dulce Impulsada por la Regulación
hook_en: Did you know the low-fat craze made us love sugar?
hook_es: ¿Sabías que la moda baja en grasa nos hizo amar el azúcar?
key_message: In 1977, regulations shifted focus to low-fat diets, leading to increased sugar use and rising obesity rates.
visual_elements: split screen of low-fat vs. high-sugar food labels
graph showing rising obesity rates from 1960-2024
_source_regulation_context:
KEY REGULATORY EVENTS:
  • 1977: McGovern Report recommends reducing sugar/fat in American diet — Impact: Food industry lobbying weakened final guidelines — focus shifted to low-fat (which led to added sugar)

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

title_en: Hidden Dangers: How Deregulation Fuels Obesity
title_es: Peligros Ocultos: Cómo la Desregulación Alimenta la Obesidad
hook_en: What if I told you some food additives are banned abroad but not here?
hook_es: ¿Y si te dijera que algunos aditivos están prohibidos en otros países pero no aquí?
key_message: Lack of regulation on certain additives in the US contributes to the obesity epidemic, unlike stricter guidelines in other countries.
visual_elements: comparison of food labels from US vs. EU
close-up of common snacks with banned additives
highlight on obesity graph with rate increases
